items.headline: New electrical infrastructure permitted for R&D expansion at Porter Drive
items.summary: A permit was issued for significant electrical upgrades at 3210 Porter Drive to support new research and development operations, including installation of transformers and a new substation connection. The project reflects continued tech industry presence in Palo Alto's commercial areas.

items.headline: Historic house preservation challenged by four-townhome development
items.summary: A property on Crescent Avenue would add four three-story townhomes behind the existing 960-square-foot historic single-family home, requiring approval of a Resource Alteration Permit from the Heritage Preservation Commission. The project raises questions about balancing historic preservation with residential development in the area.

items.headline: Campbell adopts policy restricting federal immigration enforcement on city property
items.summary: The City Council passed a resolution prohibiting use of city-owned and operated property for federal civil immigration enforcement activities. This policy decision reflects the city's stance on immigration enforcement and limits cooperation with federal agencies on municipal property.

items.headline: City Council Approves New Digital Signage Agreement with Clear Channel
items.summary: San José approved an amendment to its agreement with Clear Channel Outdoor LLC for large format digital signage on city-owned property. This could affect where and how digital advertisements appear throughout the city.

items.headline: City Seeks New Encampment Cleanup Services Contractor
items.summary: The city issued a report on requests for proposals for encampment cleanup services, indicating it may be changing or expanding how it handles homeless encampment sites. This reflects ongoing efforts to address homelessness in San José.

items.headline: Terminal Elevator Replacement Project Moves Forward
items.summary: City Council took action on the Terminal Elevator Replacement Project, though specific details weren't provided in the agenda summary. This likely relates to infrastructure improvements at a major city facility.

items.headline: City negotiating property purchase with school district on Finch Avenue
items.summary: Cupertino is in negotiations with the Cupertino Union School District to purchase property at 10480 Finch Avenue, with discussions ongoing about price and payment terms. The outcome could affect land use and development in that area.

items.headline: Milpitas adopts Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan
items.summary: The city council approved a comprehensive master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District area. This plan will guide the development of new recreational spaces and connectivity in a major part of Milpitas.
